items.title: Man City to appeal rule barring Guéhi from final
items.summary: Manchester City are set to ask the English Football League for special permission to play Marc Guéhi in the Carabao Cup final, according to Pep Guardiola.

items.title: USMNT's Cardoso out 2 Atleti games with injury
items.summary: United States men's national team midfielder Johnny Cardoso has a low grade muscle injury sustained in Wednesday's training session and will be out for at least a week, Atlético Madrid said.

items.title: City fly past Newcastle into cup final with Arsenal
items.summary: Manchester City struck three times in the first half as they eased into the Carabao Cup final by completing a comfortable 5-1 aggregate win over Newcastle United.

items.title: Desperation rankings: Judging all 20 teams by transfer panic
items.summary: Which Premier League teams were most desperate the winter transfer window?

items.title: Each Premier League team re-ranked: Man United are good now, and Arsenal get even better
items.summary: Let's rank all 20 Premier League teams again: Man United are finally good, somehow Arsenal gets even better, and what happened to Crystal Palace?

items.title: Ergernis in de Elzas: Strasbourg voelt zich speelbal van Chelsea
items.summary: Verkeer tussen Londen en Straatsburg is er al decennialang: de Britse metropool is een politiek en financieel zwaargewicht in Europa, de Franse provinciestad al ruim zeventig jaar één van de thuisbases van de Europese Unie. Over het algemeen zorgt die uitwisseling niet voor problemen. Een nieuwe 'samenwerking' op voetbalgebied wél. RC Strasbourg en Chelsea hebben dit seizoen al dertien onderlinge transfers afgerond. In Straatsburg zijn ze het inmiddels beu, hun rol als speelbal van Stamford Bridge.

items.title: Mühren (36) stopt na dit seizoen, krijgt dan rol in technische staf Volendam
items.summary: FC Volendam moet het vanaf komend seizoen doen zonder de doelpunten van Robert Mühren. De 36-jarige aanvaller stopt aan het einde van de huidige jaargang en gaat daarna verder in een rol bij de technische staf van de huidige nummer vijftien van de Eredivisie.

items.title: Top-four seeding of Women’s FA Cup would kill the magic and widen money gap
items.summary: The FA’s proposals for a shake-up of the competition have met a groundswell of opposition with fans arguing it would bolster the wealthy elite As so much of the modern game increasingly sucks the joy out of football, there remains something pure and precious about the sight of those famous black and white numbered balls being tipped out of the velvet bag for an FA Cup draw. Your heart rate intensifies as they clatter when tipped into the bowl. Each side has the same chance of being pitted against any other club and, for those few moments, there is a special feeling. Hope. Continue reading...

items.title: Waar ligt de toekomst van Sano?
items.summary: NEC-watcher Sander Janssen gaat in op de laatste ontwikkelingen rondom Kodai Sano. Hij schetst hoe NEC naar de ontwikkeling van de gewilde middenvelder kijkt. Een transfer deze zomer lijkt volgens Janssen dan ook zeer realistisch, maar de grote vraag is vooral: welke stap past het best bij hem? Blijft hij nog binnen de Eredivisie of lonkt het buitenland?

items.title: Bloedvorm erkend: Summerville maakt kans op fraaie onderscheiding
items.summary: Crysencio Summerville maakt kans op de prijs voor Speler van de Maand in de Premier League, zo werd donderdag bekend. De Nederlandse aanvaller van West Ham United behoort tot de acht genomineerden voor januari.

items.title: Lof voor Mauro Júnior: 'Bosz zou er wel twee willen'
items.summary: PSV-watcher Tim Reedijk schijnt zijn licht op Mauro Júnior, die volgens hem van onschatbare waarde is voor het elftal. Niet altijd de meest opvallende naam op het veld, maar juist in de luwte blijkt hij cruciaal in het spel van PSV. Reedijk bespreekt wat Mauro Júnior zo belangrijk maakt voor het team, waarom trainers zo dol op hem zijn en hoe zijn rol zich de afgelopen periode heeft ontwikkeld. Ook komt zijn toekomst aan bod: blijft hij deze stille kracht in Eindhoven, of lonkt er interesse van buitenaf en kijkt Koeman mee?

items.title: Horneland a leading candidate for Aberdeen job
items.summary: Eirik Horneland has emerged as a leading candidate for the manager's job at Aberdeen.

items.title: Are Chelsea-Strasbourg transfer deals bad for football?
items.summary: Chelsea and Strasbourg have completed 12 transfer deals this summer. Is it out of the ordinary, and might Fifa get involved?

items.title: Why every EFL kick-off time will be one minute later this weekend
items.summary: All EFL matches this weekend - from February 5-9 - will kick off one minute late to raise awareness of the 'Every Minute Matters' campaign.

items.title: Why Jérémy Jacquet may be the ‘heir to Virgil van Dijk’ for Liverpool
items.summary: The 20-year-old is a ‘complete’ player who combines Marcel Desailly’s defensive ability with Laurent Blanc’s creativity By Get French Football News When you miss 18 months of football, there is a natural eagerness to make up for lost time. Jérémy Jacquet has certainly done that. This time last year Rennes recalled him from a loan spell at Ligue 2 side Clermont Foot and now he has been signed by Liverpool for £60m. The conditions were always ripe for Jacquet to succeed at Rennes, a club known for developing talent. But even by their standards, the 2005 generation is something special. Désiré Doué, Mathys Tel, Jeanuël Belocian, Lesley Ugochukwu and, come the summer, Jacquet will have all left Rennes, but not before pushing each other to greater heights during their formative years. Continue reading...

items.title: In-form Reed makes storming start to Qatar Masters
items.summary: Patrick Reed continued his storming start to the year on the DP World Tour by carding a five-under 65 to share the clubhouse lead in the opening round of the Qatar Masters.

items.title: Man Utd prepare to honour Munich disaster victims
items.summary: It is impossible to fully understand the magnitude of the crash that killed 23 people, eight of them Manchester United players, until you work for the club, says women's coach Marc Skinner.

items.title: EFL will knock back Guardiola’s push for Guéhi to play in Carabao Cup final
items.summary: Cup rules state new signings must arrive before semi ‘Hopefully they can change it,’ City manager said Any attempt by Manchester City to clear Marc Guéhi to play in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal will be rebuffed by the English Football League. Guardiola questioned the rule which means Guéhi cannot feature in March’s final and said City would ask the EFL to reconsider. But there will be no change to a regulation introduced before this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Townsend hits out at attempt to 'disrupt' Scotland with Newcastle links
items.summary: Gregor Townsend dismissed claims that he has been primed to take over at Newcastle Red Bulls as an attempt to "disrupt" Scotland ahead of the Six Nations as he named a much-changed side for their opener against Italy on Saturday.

items.title: Celtic in advanced talks over Oxlade-Chamberlain
items.summary: Celtic are in advanced talks with former Arsenal and Liverpool midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain.
